settings.text:
Men’s jewelry has a rich history, carrying meanings of power, loyalty, and individual identity through time. Each era has contributed unique styles and symbolism, from ancient protective amulets to sleek, contemporary designs. 

MARCO DAL MASO captures the spirit of these historical influences, reinventing them in sophisticated collections that reflect the journey of men’s jewelry across centuries.

Ancient Times: Power and Protection

In ancient Egypt, Greece, and Rome, jewelry held symbolic power. Pharaohs wore protective amulets, while Greek warriors adorned themselves with talismans that invoked bravery in battle. 
Disover MARCO DAL MASO pendants for men, that reflect traditions of strength and resilience.

Medieval and Renaissance Eras: Faith and Allegiance

Throughout the Middle Ages, jewelry became a means to showcase faith, loyalty, and social status. Kings, knights, and nobles wore rings with family crests or religious symbols, and Renaissance jewelry brought forth even more intricate craftsmanship. 
The MONETA Collection captures this sense of opulence and tradition, inspired by the historic coins of Venice. Just as medieval jewelry pieces symbolized allegiance and authority, the Moneta pieces carry a regal connection to history.

settings.code:
18th and 19th Centuries: Sentimentality and Personal Expression

With the Industrial Revolution, jewelry was no longer exclusive to the wealthy. In the 19th century, men’s jewelry gained sentimental value, with lockets, rings, and other pieces reflecting personal relationships and memories.

Early 20th Century: Bold Statements and Art Deco Influence

The Art Deco movement of the early 20th century embraced sleek lines and bold statements, with men’s jewelry reflecting this new era of style and individual expression.  MARCO DAL MASO’s DE STIJL Collection embodies this fusion of classic and daring design, drawing inspiration from the minimalist Stijl movement. These pieces capture the balance of elegance and modernity, much like the Art Deco jewelry of the 1920s.

custom_css: .custom__item-inner p {color: #111; font-size: var(--typeBaseSize); font-weight: 400; letter-spacing: var(--typeBaseSpacing); line-height: var(--typeBaseLineHeight); text-rendering: optimizeSpeed;}

settings.text:
Mid-20th Century: Cultural Influence and Rebellion

By the 1960s and 70s, jewelry became a powerful tool for self-expression, breaking traditional molds with bold designs and unique materials. 
The GLADIATOR Collection channels the rebellious spirit of this era, with pieces crafted from vegan leather and adorned with lab-grown diamonds. Reflecting the countercultural movement’s ideals, this collection is a testament to ethical luxury and standing out with pride.

Modern Era: Refined Sophistication and Individuality

Today’s men’s jewelry embraces individual style and sleek sophistication. Minimalist yet impactful, modern pieces are crafted to express personality and elegance.
MARCO DAL MASO’s ACIES Collection is the embodiment of this modern ethos., ACIES symbolizes the parallel yet distinct paths we walk, celebrating individuality while connecting us through refined craftsmanship. Explore now.


From ancient symbols of protection to contemporary expressions of individuality, men’s jewelry has traversed many eras and styles. MARCO DAL MASO’s collections honor this journey, blending historical inspiration with modern elegance.
